title: Iron Snake Mode: Random Board Shapes & Level Goals
content:
## Overview

Iron Snake Mode is an optional game mode (default off) where the board is carved into a random, irregular shape instead of the usual square grid. The grid-doubling mechanic (`expandGrid`) is replaced by a level/goal system: each level has a cumulative score threshold; when reached, a fresh random shape is generated, snakes are repositioned, and a higher goal is set.

The mode composes with all three existing game modes (single-player, two-player, bot-vs-bot). When the toggle is off, the classic game is byte-for-byte unchanged.

---

## Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/iron-snake.ts` | Board mask generator (pure, no game-state imports) |
| `src/main.ts` | State fields, `onBoard()`, `inBounds()`, level/goal system, placement, HUD, toggle wiring |
| `index.html` | Menu toggle (bottom of button group, below "Bot vs Bot"), HUD countdown element |
| `src/style.css` | Iron Snake Mode toggle styling |

---

## Board Mask & State (`src/main.ts`)

New state fields:
- `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` ‚Äî flat array of length `gridSize¬≤`; `1` = playable, `0` = off-board; `null` = classic full rectangle.
- `ironSnakeMode: boolean` ‚Äî the menu toggle; persists across replays.
- `boardArea: number` ‚Äî count of playable cells (replaces `gridSize¬≤` as capacity metric).
- `levelGoal: number` ‚Äî current cumulative score threshold.

`onBoard(x, y)` is the single source of truth for playable cells:
```
rectangle bounds AND (boardMask === null OR boardMask[y*gridSize + x] === 1)
```

`inBounds(position)` is rewritten to delegate entirely to `onBoard(position.x, position.y)`. Because every collision path (player movement, `wouldCollide`, `checkCollision`, bot BFS `analyzePosition`) already funnels through `inBounds`, the irregular shape becomes an automatic constraint everywhere with no changes to bot logic or movement loops.

---

## Shape Generator (`src/iron-snake.ts`)

Exports `generateIronSnakeMask(gridSize, rng?) ‚Üí { mask: Uint8Array; area: number } | null`.

**Algorithm ‚Äî overlapping rooms:**
1. Place 3‚Äì6 rectangles of minimum side 3, each overlapping at least one existing on-board cell (guaranteeing connectivity between rooms).
2. Keep only the largest 4-connected component via flood fill (BFS).
3. Reject if fill ratio is outside the target band (area control).
4. Reject if a 3√ó3 morphological open (erode then dilate) removes any cell ‚Äî any removed cell sat in a corridor narrower than 3 cells.
5. Retry up to 30 times; return `null` on exhaustion so callers always fall back to a full rectangle.

Validated across 8,000 trials with 100% connectivity and zero corridor failures.

**Why overlapping rooms over alternatives:**
- Rectangles of min side ‚â• 3 make the no-narrow-corridor property nearly free by construction.
- Overlap guarantees connectivity without extra wiring.
- Area is easy to tune.
- Cellular automata caves and random-walk accretion were rejected as harder to control for minimum corridor width and fill ratio without extensive parameter tuning.

---

## Level / Goal System

Replaces `checkGridExpansion()` call sites (single-player ~line 1015, multiplayer ~line 1084) when Iron Snake Mode is active.

**Goal formula:** `5¬∑L(L+1)/2` ‚Äî cumulative thresholds of 5, 15, 30, 50, 75, ‚Ä¶ with per-level deltas of 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, ‚Ä¶ Each level is strictly harder than the previous by a fixed additional amount.

**Advance condition:**
- Single-player: `score >= levelGoal`
- Multiplayer: `score + score2 >= levelGoal` (combined score)

**On level advance (`advanceLevel()`):**
1. `level++`
2. Regenerate board mask (fall back to `null` on generator failure).
3. Reposition snake(s) to length 1 via `findStartCell()`.
4. `spawnFood()`
5. `levelGoal = goalForLevel(level)`
6. Ramp speed gently at 10% per level (`baseSpeed * 0.9^(level-1)`, floored) rather than halving.
7. Restart the game interval; call `updateUI()`.

`gridSize` does **not** change in Iron Snake Mode, so the canvas never resizes and `isoCache` stays valid.

**Why cumulative thresholds over per-level resets:** Goals are thresholds on the running total score. The leaderboard score is always the true final score, each goal is crossed exactly once, and there is no confusing mid-game score reset. The trade-off is that early levels feel shorter since the snake already has momentum.

---

## Snake Placement (`findStartCell`)

`findStartCell(preferredRegion, occupied) ‚Üí { pos, dir }` picks an on-board, unoccupied cell nearest `preferredRegion` with a direction giving ‚â• `FORWARD_CLEARANCE` (~3) clear cells ahead, preventing immediate wall collisions.

- Single-player: centroid of the board.
- Multiplayer P1: left-third centroid; P2: right-third centroid with P1's cells marked occupied.
- Mutual reachability is guaranteed ‚Äî the mask is always a single connected component.
- Falls back to classic rectangle placement if `findStartCell` returns null.

---

## HUD

When Iron Snake Mode is active, the grid-size display (`#grid-size`) is replaced by a live countdown: **"To goal: N pts / 1 pt"** that decrements as the player eats and resets on level advance. This works for both single-player and the multiplayer combined-score case with no extra UI.

---

## Food Spawning

`spawnFood` uses `onBoard` in both the empty-cell filter (high-fill branch) and the rejection-sample `while` condition. The branch selector compares against `boardArea` rather than `gridSize¬≤`.

---

## Rendering

- `drawGroundPlane`: skips cells where `!onBoard(gx, gy)` ‚Äî void cells show the dark background.
- `drawGridLines`: when mask is active, strokes per-cell edges (deduplicating shared edges) instead of full spans.
- `drawGroundBorder`: when mask is active, strokes every edge where an on-board cell meets an off-board cell or the rectangle boundary, tracing the true irregular outline.
- `drawBlock` / `getBlockHeight` / `isoCache` are unchanged ‚Äî `isoCache` covers the full `(gridSize+1)¬≤` lattice and is valid for any subset.

---

## Bots

Bots are **shape-unaware** ‚Äî `BotState` (`bot-types.ts`) is unchanged. Bot moves are validated through the mask-aware `inBounds`, so a stale heuristic kills the bot rather than corrupting state. Edge-hugging bots (explorer, edge-runner, spiral, sweeper, ambusher, zigzag) degrade gracefully on irregular shapes. This is acceptable per requirements.

---

## Leaderboard

Iron Snake Mode runs record into the same `single|pvp|bvb` leaderboard buckets (`gameMode` is unchanged). No changes to `src/leaderboard.ts`. Tagging entries with `ironSnake: true` is a possible follow-up.

---

## Naming History

The feature went through two user-driven renames: "Random Arena" ‚Üí "Iron Man Mode" ‚Üí **"Iron Snake Mode"**. All internal identifiers (element ids, CSS classes, state fields, module name, exported function names, constants) were renamed from `arena`/`randomArena` to `ironSnake`/`ironSnakeMode` in one pass. A `grep -i arena` check confirmed zero mixed vocabulary remains.
